838 PORT - Portuguese

PLSC805 Advanced Crop Physiology (2 Credits)

Major emphasis will be on physiological processes affecting yield

and productivity of major food fiber and industrial crops of the world. Topics such as photosynthesis, respiration, photorespiration, nitrogen metabolism will be related to crop growth as affected by management decisions. Topics of discussion will also include growth analysis and the use of computer modeling of crop growth by plant scientists. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in PLSC400; or minimum grade of C-in BSCI442; and must have completed advanced training in plant sciences.
